articles.title: Who is Aparna Yadav: Prateek Yadav’s wife and ‘chhoti bahu’ of UP’s influential Yadav clan in focus
articles.description: Prateek Yadav, son of Mulayam Singh Yadav and stepbrother of Akhilesh Yadav, passed away at 38 in Lucknow. His wife, Aparna Bisht Yadav, a former SP candidate who later joined the BJP, currently serves as vice-chairperson of the State Women Commission. Prateek had previously accused Aparna of damaging family relations.

articles.title: West Bengal BJP president Samik Bhattacharya says new government will adopt new land policy to bring industries
articles.description: BJP leader Samik Bhattacharya promises new land policies in West Bengal to attract industries and enhance economic growth.

articles.description: An 8-year-old boy stunned viewers after performing a difficult yoga pose while reciting the Shiv Tandav Stotram and setting a new world record.

articles.title: Kidney health in India: The food and hydration mistakes doctor wants you to avoid
articles.description: Kidneys often go unnoticed until problems arise. Dr. Harsha Mandadi Varadaraju highlights that aging kidneys don't have to decline; lifestyle choices, especially diet, are crucial. Excessive salt intake, a common issue in Indian households, significantly impacts blood pressure and kidney health. Staying hydrated is vital, particularly in regions prone to kidney stones.

articles.title: Googlebook unveiled: An AI-first laptop powered by Google Gemini; How does it work & when will it be available?
articles.description: Googlebook Gemini intelligence laptops: Google has unveiled “Googlebook,” a new category of AI-first laptops designed around Gemini Intelligence. Built using parts of Android and ChromeOS, the company says these laptops will offer proactive AI assistance, deep phone integration, and a more personalised computing experience.

articles.title: How a global retailer and banker are using AI
articles.description: AI is driving a fundamental shift in business, forcing companies like Lowe's and Barclays to reimagine customer experience, employee productivity, and business processes. Lowe's uses AI assistants for store associates and customers, seeing significant improvements in engagement and sales. Barclays leverages AI for faster decision-making in banking and is now exploring end-to-end process reinvention and new business opportunities.

articles.title: Anthony Volpe’s girlfriend Elle Jowett drops stunning modeling photos as Yankees star earns major promotion
articles.description: Anthony Volpe is set to rejoin the New York Yankees after recovering from a shoulder injury, though his return comes amid growing debate over his offensive struggles. Injuries in the Yankees infield created an opening despite an underwhelming Triple-A stint. At the same time, Volpe’s girlfriend, Elle Jowett, gained attention online after sharing modeling photos that drew praise from several members of the Yankees family circle.

articles.title: MLB trade rumors: Bob Nightengale reveals four SF Giants stars who could soon be traded
articles.description: The San Francisco Giants may be preparing for major roster changes after trading Patrick Bailey to the Cleveland Guardians. USA Today insider Bob Nightengale reported that the club would like to move Rafael Devers, Matt Chapman, Willy Adames and Jung Hoo Lee as part of a possible reset. The report has increased speculation around the Giants’ future before the 2026 MLB Draft.

articles.title: US stocks fall, oil rallies as Middle East peace hopes fade
articles.description: The U.S. war on Iran has driven oil prices higher, resulting in more expensive gasoline, diesel and jet fuel, and economists expect to see second-round effects in the months ahead.

articles.title: 'People's will through lawmakers can bring religious reforms': Supreme Court
articles.description: Constitutional courts should be extremely reluctant to alter religious practices, a nine-judge Supreme Court bench cautioned. Only the will of the people, expressed through elected representatives in Parliament and assemblies, can bring about reforms in religious customs. The court emphasized that it's impossible to scrutinize every religious practice.

articles.title: Govt to introduce new system for ‘raiyati’ land deals soon
articles.description: In a pioneering effort to bolster land transaction integrity, Bihar has launched a cutting-edge e-registration system. This innovation allows prospective buyers to easily access comprehensive official land records prior to the registration of raiyati land. Aimed at reducing fraud and legal disputes, this initiative is part of the larger 'Sabka Samman, Jeevan Aasaan' initiative.

articles.title: 3 persons attacked with swords in Baranga
articles.description: Three individuals were attacked with swords by four assailants on motorcycles near Munduli canal chowk in Cuttack. The victims, identified as Kahnu Dalei, Batua Rout, and Anil Lenka, sustained serious injuries and are in stable condition. Police suspect past enmity as the motive and are reviewing CCTV footage to identify the attackers.

articles.title: West Asia crisis a wake-up call for self-reliance: Commerce and industry minister Piyush Goyal
articles.description: The West Asia crisis serves as a crucial reminder for Indian industries to boost self-reliance and efficiency. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal urged businesses to reduce import dependency and focus on indigenous design and manufacturing. He also highlighted the need for faster electric vehicle adoption to cut oil bills.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Christopher Paolini: 'The trick is to find happiness in the brief gaps between...' Life lessons on joy, human nature and hardships by American writer
articles.description: Quote of the day by Christopher Paolini highlights a message about finding happiness during short calm moments between life’s challenges. The quote comes from the novel Brisingr, part of The Inheritance Cycle. It reflects resilience, human struggle, and the search for joy despite uncertainty. The message connects with readers across ages because it explains how people can value brief moments of peace while facing ongoing problems in daily life.
